commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Judge what they have judged unjustly. (Worthington)

commentaries.author: Thomas Aquinas
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh O LORD, you have seen my wrong: judge you my cause. Here the judge and his justice is commended: "Thou hast taken up my cause, O Lord, thou hast redeemed my life. " That is, during past times. As concurs Psalm 43 (42):l: "Vindicate me, O God, and defend my cause against an ungodly people. " Then Verse 59 continues: "Thou hast seen the wrong done to me, O Lord; judge thou my cause. " The benevolence of the divine judge is here accounted for.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
